steps.title: Recognize the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution exactly when the two lines are parallel — they have the same slope but different y-intercepts, so they never intersect.

steps.title: Find the slope of the given equation
steps.content:
The given equation has the form $7x - 27ry = c$ (a constant on the \right). Solve for $y$ to put it in slope-intercept form:
$$-27ry = -7x + c$$$$y = \frac{7}{27r}\,x - \frac{c}{27r}$$The slope of the given line is $\frac{7}{27r}$.

steps.title: Determine the slope of the other equation
steps.content: For no solution, the other line must be parallel to the given line, so it must share the same slope. The other equation therefore has slope $\frac{7}{27r}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
